section.title: Inside your organization
section.intro: How organizations actually adopt AI — the gap between leadership enthusiasm and staff experience, and what to do about it.

resources.title: Overcoming Organizational Barriers to AI Adoption
resources.author: Harvard Business Review
resources.description: Employee-centric organizations are 7x more likely to succeed with AI adoption than top-down mandates. Survey of 1,000+ organizations.

resources.title: AI Adoption Accelerating, Confidence Collapsing
resources.description: Trust in company AI programs fell 31% in three months. Adoption accelerated at the same time.

resources.title: Fast Forward AI Policy Builder
resources.author: Fast Forward
resources.description: Step-by-step questionnaire that produces a customized draft AI policy. Covers data classification, approved tools, training requirements, and governance structure.

resources.title: AI and the Politics of Avoidance in Global Health
resources.author: Leah Shipton & Lucia Vitale
resources.description: Most global health organizations know AI is here and have chosen not to engage. That avoidance is itself a political act — it preserves existing power structures by default.

resources.title: Why Gen AI Feels So Threatening to Workers
resources.description: Three threats beyond job loss: competence (AI approximates expert output), autonomy (decisions shift to systems workers don't control), belonging (team identity shifts when core tasks change).

resources.title: The Emerging Agentic Enterprise
resources.description: Four tensions of agentic AI adoption: delegation vs. control, speed vs. oversight, capability vs. trust, individual vs. institutional.

resources.title: AI Readiness for Nonprofits
resources.author: NTEN
resources.description: Organizational readiness assessment: culture, capacity, and governance.

resources.title: AI Policy Template for Nonprofits
resources.author: Community IT
resources.description: Customizable template with data classification framework. Designed for organizations without dedicated IT policy staff.

callout.title: The enthusiasm gap
callout.body: 76% of executives think their employees are enthusiastic about AI. 31% actually are. Organizations that deploy AI tools without employee input see sharper confidence drops and higher resistance. (BCG/HBR, Nov 2025)
